title: THREE KINDS OF FAITH  | BG 17.01 - 17.03 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: So the topic is three kinds of faith, based on the teachings of His Divine Grace A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada.
This is text 17.01. Arjuna said,
O Krishna, what is the situation of one who does not follow the principles of scriptures but worships according to his own imagination?
Is he in goodness, in passion or in ignorance?
Anyone can help me with the name of the chapter?
The divisions of faith. So this chapter predominantly talks about faith.
And when we're talking about three kinds of faith,
faith in the mode of goodness, faith in the mode of passion, faith in the mode of ignorance.
So in this chapter, we'll be hearing Krishna extensively talking about the influence of modes.
We will get to understand foods in the mode of goodness, food in the mode of passion, food in the mode of ignorance.
Sacrifice in the mode of goodness, in the mode of passion, in the mode of ignorance.
Faith in the matter of goodness.
So just to refresh our understanding, we all are getting influenced by the modes of material nature.
And they are predominantly how many number?
Three.
So just like in one of the earlier session, we talked about that how in printing industry using four colors,
we can create unlimited number of shades.
And I remember we talked about the dots C, M, Y, K.
So different permutation and combination of influence of modes influencing a person
results in different natures, different behaviors.
If a person is predominantly influenced by rajoguna,
his nature will be one type, his activities will be one type, his choice of food will be one type,
his faith will be of one type.
If a person is predominantly influenced by sattoguna, accordingly, his choice of food,
his taste, his nature, his inclination.
The kind of worship he does will be different.
And Krishna says in the Gita that we helplessly act under the influence of the modes of nature.
And there is some kind of a competition between these modes.
Sometimes mode of sattoguna, which is goodness, overpowers the other two.
We are predominantly under the influence of sattoguna.
Anyone can help me, what time of the day generally sattoguna is predominant?
Morning time.
If you also notice, if you have had a good sleep, when you wake up in the morning, you are fresh.
You are more calm.
You are more relaxed.
Day time, post noon is rajoguna, which is predominant.
And night time it is tamoguna.
That's why you see activities also.
Morning time, the temples open.
You will have all the temples, morning, 5 o'clock, 6 o'clock, the mangal aarti, darshan aarti and all that.
You will hardly find temple having aartis at 10.30 in the night.
You will hardly find any pubs, bars, operational at 6 am in the morning, opening.
You will hardly find, you know, as I said, pubs, bars, invariably open.
Or other function late in the evenings or night.
So the divisions of faith based on the influence of modes of material nature.
Krishna also in the says, Gita says, prakriti kriyamani.
Prakriti means this material nature, which has three modes.
Prakriti kriyamani, according to the modes, gunai karmani sarvasha.
These gunas are the resultant.
The cause of all the actions we perform.
They are the ones who are goading us or pushing us to act in a particular way.
Gunai karmani sarvasha.
Karmani means actions.
Sarvasha means all.
Prakriti kriyamani.
Gunai karmani sarvasha.

We think we are the doers.
So Vedic civilization.
The Vedic culture is all about cultivating higher modes.
So if a person is in tamo guna predominantly,
progress for him, advancement for him is where the percentage of influence of tamo guna reduces and the percentage of other gunas increase.
He is well situated.
He is well situated if he is predominantly under the influence of tamo guna.

two year old, three year old, boy or girl they are no attraction in that sense. It's
dormant. But when they attain certain age it becomes dormant.
triggered. So likewise, this attraction to the Lord, desire to serve the Lord, hear about
the Lord, eagerness to serve the Lord is dormant in us. Just that, it is covered with all the
dirty things. And hence, even though that desire is there, it is not awakened. But to
remove the dirty things, all the dirty things you remove, it comes out. That's why Prabhupada
said, when he went to the West, they were very degraded people, all these drug addicts,
hippies, no idea of Indian culture, not heard about Krishna. They started chanting Hare
Krishna and all these things, the desire to serve the Lord, desire to know about the Lord,
hear about the Lord, eagerness.
Just like at the right age, the desire's attraction gets triggered. Likewise, you
attain certain level of purification, destruction of abhadras, this becomes awakened. So it
is not artificial. Krishna consciousness, desire to serve the Lord, chant the names
of the Lord, hear about the Lord, you are not putting it from outside, it is there within
us.
But some people have no desire to hear about the Lord because it is very covered.
Once you remove that covering, just like a mirror, when it is covered with dust, you
cannot see. Thick layer of dust, you cannot see anything. You remove the dust, you can
see.
So this process of Krishna consciousness, this process of purification, we are removing
all the unheard thoughts, all the unwanted things which has covered our consciousness,
the lower modes, tamah and rajah, which has covered us. You remove all that and then
pure consciousness comes.
Narada Muni, in his previous life was anybody can know?
Son of a maid servant.
Son of a maid servant.
He was not a scholar, he was not a pundit..
He was not a Vedantist. He was son of a maidservant. And during Chaturmas, now the Chaturmas is the last month, Chaturmas, generally sages used to travel from one place to another.

So they would camp in one particular place because it was a rainy season time and invariably when they would go through forest, it could be very muddy, it could be very, you know, the puddles of water and snakes and all that.
So they would camp in one particular place during Chaturmas. So Narada Muni, one time when these sages were camping, his mother, being a maidservant, used to cook for them and serve them the meals.

And while Narada Muni was there, when they were discussing about the Lord something, he would patiently sit and hear. And after the mother has served, the remnants of the food what these sages have eaten, he would take the remnants as prasad.

And just by hearing these sages, although he would have limitation as to, as being a child, how much he could understand, and by taking the food which was partaken by exalted personalities, maha prasad, he became a Vedantist.
Next life he became Narada Muni.
So coming back to the question, the Lord is very merciful. People may be in very fallen conditions.
If they get the mercy of the Lord, predominantly in three forms, one is through his holy names, one is through the sanctified food, prasad, and third is through knowledge, divya gyana.
Even that person can be elevated.
Now somebody predominantly can be elevated, he is illiterate, so may not be through knowledge. He does not have food to eat, may not be through knowledge.
People say, if he does not have food in his pocket, then what will he hear about the Lord. Yes, it is a fact.
For him, the predominant way for elevation is prasad.
So we find different types of faith in the world, different religions, different forms of worship.
That is why we see there is so much of different people worshipping different gods,
having different religious beliefs, having different faiths, different forms of worship.
It is all because of different kinds of modes influencing them.
Different kinds of abhadras, different kinds of material desires which they are desiring to fulfil through that worship.

which is going to happen to many of us.
So what does Krishna say?
He says he will take birth in family of devotees.
Or rich aristocracy, where he will continue from where he has left and he will naturally get attracted to religious principles.
You see, it is a proof here.
You see, we would have touched not less than 5,000, 10,000 people.
But why handful of people have got attracted and they feel inspired to come week after week.
Somewhere we have left our journey in last life, somewhere, possibly.
Some people among us are chanting 16 rounds, some people are chanting 8 rounds, some are chanting 2 rounds, some are chanting 1 round,
based on where we have left our journey in the previous life.
That's how we see there is so much of disparity.
So, and every one of us are, will find ourselves in fortunate or unfortunate circumstances
based on the choices we have made in this life.
Prabhupada gave his own example.
Right from his very birth, he was born in a family of Vaishnavas.
His father was encouraging him to do Rathiyatra.
They were offering food in the house.
They were having Prasadam.
Just by imitating his father, doing some activities, holding Rathiyatra, like a childhood game.
They were all getting elevated.
So we all, that's why it is said there is no loss or diminution.
Even if we don't complete this journey, whatever we are gaining is our permanent credit.
So we have to gain as much as we can.
Don't blame circumstances.
Don't say, my situation is not good.
Whatever your situation, do the best you can and get the situation.
God is watching.
